widget.name: Convey Modal Popup
widget.description: Dockable side modal with Data view–like content, header drag repositioning, shared bottom dock, side-by-side multi-open layout, and configurable size, colors, and borders.

property.caption: Title
property.description: Text shown in the maximized panel header and on the minimized dock tab. Supports static text, expressions, and attribute values (for example 'Address Change' or $Request/Name). If empty at runtime, the widget falls back to the label 'Modal'. Use a clear, short title so dock tabs stay readable when several modals are minimized.

property.caption: Trigger
property.description: Drop zone for widgets that open the modal when clicked (for example an Action Button, Icon, or Image). Configure Trigger, Is open, or both. Nested clickable widgets inside the trigger also open the modal. If the trigger contains its own button or link, the wrapper does not add an extra keyboard role, so accessibility stays correct. Leave empty when opening is driven only by the Is open attribute from a microflow or nanoflow.

property.caption: Content
property.description: Drop zone for the panel body. Place Text boxes, buttons, or any page widgets here. To give Content an object context, place this widget inside a Data view (or nest a Data view inside Content): nested widgets then bind that object's attributes exactly as in a Data view. Content stays mounted while the modal is minimized so typed values are preserved until the modal is fully closed. In design mode, drop, paste, or move widgets into this zone the same way as a Container.

property.caption: Is open
property.description: Optional Boolean attribute for two-way open/close control from microflows or nanoflows. Set true to open maximized; set false to close and remove the dock tab. The widget also writes to this attribute when the user opens or closes the modal (header Close, dock Close, Escape, or overlay click when enabled). Place the widget in a context that provides this attribute (for example inside a Data view). Prefer a stable helper object (page parameter or committed non-persistent entity) that is not recreated on every field change. After a short Data view remount, the widget restores open/minimized state and may re-assert true if the new object still has false.

property.caption: Dock position
property.description: Home edge of the viewport where the maximized panel appears before any drag. Right/Left dock to the side edges; Top/Bottom dock to the top or bottom edge. For Top or Bottom, use Horizontal align to pin the panel to the left or right corner of that edge. When When multiple open is Open beside, only panels on the same edge and align corner are tiled next to each other. Drag still moves the panel from this home position using a pixel offset. Top offset applies to Left, Right, and Top docks.

property.caption: Horizontal align
property.description: When Dock position is Top or Bottom, anchors the panel to the Left or Right corner of that edge (for example Bottom + Right = bottom-right). Ignored when Dock position is Left or Right. Open beside tiles additional panels horizontally from this corner.

property.caption: Width
property.description: CSS width of the maximized panel. Examples: 480px, 40%, 30vw. Required. Used for layout and for side-by-side inset calculation (the widget measures the rendered pixel width after layout). Prefer consistent widths across multiple open modals when using Open beside so tiles line up cleanly.

property.caption: Height
property.description: CSS height of the maximized panel. Examples: 100%, 90vh, 800px. Required. Use with Top offset when the panel should not cover the full viewport height (for example under a top navigation bar). The panel body scrolls if Content exceeds this height.

property.caption: Top offset
property.description: CSS top position of the maximized panel when Dock position is Left, Right, or Top. Examples: 0, 64px, 10vh. Use this to clear a top navigation bar or app header. Ignored when Dock position is Bottom.

property.caption: Z-index
property.description: Stacking order for the overlay and panel. The overlay uses this value; the panel uses Z-index + 1. The shared dock bar uses a fixed z-index of 1100. Keep the panel Z-index below 1100 if dock tabs must stay clickable above the panel. Raise it only when the panel must appear above other page overlays that use a similar range.

property.caption: Enable drag
property.description: When true, the user can drag the maximized panel by its header to any position on the screen. Form fields in Content remain usable (only the header is the drag handle; Minimize and Close never start a drag). The dragged position is kept while minimized and when restoring, and resets to the docked home position when the modal is closed. The panel is clamped so about 40px of the header stays on-screen, including after window resize. Set false to keep the panel fixed to Dock position (plus side-by-side inset when applicable).

property.caption: When multiple open
property.description: How maximized panels are arranged when more than one Convey Modal Popup is open on the same dock edge. Open beside (default) places each new panel next to earlier ones: the first sits at the dock edge, the next is offset by the previous panel size plus an 8px gap (width for Left/Right, height for Top/Bottom), and so on. Closing or minimizing a panel reflows the remaining ones. If combined sizes exceed the viewport, inset is capped so about 40px of the panel stays visible. Overlap stacks every maximized panel at the dock edge. Each dock edge (left, right, top, bottom) is arranged independently. Drag offsets still apply on top of the home inset.

property.caption: Icon class
property.description: Optional CSS class name for an icon shown before the title in the maximized header and on the minimized dock tab. Example from Atlas/Glyphicons: glyphicon glyphicon-file. Leave empty for no icon. The icon uses Title color in the header and Tab text color on the dock tab.

property.caption: Show overlay
property.description: When true, a dimmed overlay covers the page behind the maximized panel and keyboard focus is trapped inside the panel (true modal behavior). When false, the page remains fully visible and interactive under the panel, focus is not trapped, and Close on overlay click has no effect. Prefer false when users must keep editing the page while the panel is open.

property.caption: Close on overlay click
property.description: When true and Show overlay is enabled, clicking the dimmed overlay closes the modal the same way as the header Close button (fires On close and sets Is open to false when bound). Default is false so accidental clicks on the page dimmer do not dismiss the panel. Ignored when Show overlay is false.

property.caption: Overlay color
property.description: Background color of the page dimmer. Accepts any CSS color: #000000, rgb(0,0,0), or rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.12). Lower alpha = lighter overlay. Only visible when Show overlay is true.

property.caption: Panel background
property.description: Background color of the maximized panel shell (outer container). Accepts hex, rgb, or rgba. Example: #ffffff. Usually kept the same as Header background and Body background for a flat look, or varied slightly for contrast.

property.caption: Header background
property.description: Background color of the panel header bar (title, optional icon, Minimize, and Close). Example: #ffffff or #f8fafc. Pair with Title color and Control color for readable branding.

property.caption: Body background
property.description: Background color of the scrollable content area below the header. Example: #ffffff or #f5f7f9. Use a light tint if Content forms should sit on a subtle surface distinct from the header.

property.caption: Title color
property.description: Text color for the panel title and optional header icon. Example: #1a2b4b. Choose a color with strong contrast against Header background.

property.caption: Control color
property.description: Color of the Minimize (−) and Close (×) icons in the panel header. Example: #1a2b4b. Does not affect dock tab icons (those use Tab text color).

property.caption: Header border color
property.description: Color of the 1px divider line under the header that separates the title bar from the body. Example: #e8eaed.

property.caption: Panel border color
property.description: Border color around the maximized panel. Only visible when Panel border width is greater than 0. Example: #e5e7eb or #2b5ce7.

property.caption: Panel border width
property.description: CSS border width for the maximized panel. Use 0 or 0px for no border; use 1px or 2px to show a border with Panel border color. Useful when the panel sits over a busy page and needs a clearer edge.

property.caption: Panel border radius
property.description: CSS border-radius for the maximized panel corners. Use 0 for a flush side panel that meets the viewport edge; use 8px or 12px for rounded corners when the panel is floated or dragged away from the edge.

property.caption: Panel box shadow
property.description: Optional CSS box-shadow override for the maximized panel. Leave empty to keep the default edge shadow (direction depends on Dock position: left, right, top, or bottom). Example: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.18). Use a stronger shadow when the panel is dragged over content and needs more elevation.

property.caption: Tab background
property.description: Background (fill) color of the minimized dock tab pill. Use different pastel colors per modal instance so users can tell tabs apart when several modals are minimized. Examples: #cfe8ff, #ffe4c4, #d4edda, #f8d7da.

property.caption: Tab text color
property.description: Color of the dock tab title text, optional icon, and the Maximize / Close icons. Example: #1a2b4b. Choose a dark color against a light pastel Tab background for readability.

property.caption: Tab border color
property.description: Border color of the minimized dock tab. Only visible when Tab border width is greater than 0. Example: rgba(26, 43, 75, 0.06) or #cbd5e1.

property.caption: Tab border width
property.description: CSS border width for the dock tab. Use 0 or 0px for no border; use 1px (default) for a light outline that separates pastel tabs from the page background.

property.caption: Tab border radius
property.description: CSS border-radius for the dock tab pill. Examples: 8px, 12px, 999px (fully rounded pill). Higher values make tabs look more like soft chips in the shared bottom dock.

property.caption: On open
property.description: Action (microflow, nanoflow, or Call nanoflow) executed when the modal opens from a closed state (trigger click or Is open set to true). Not fired when restoring from minimized, and not fired again if the widget only re-asserts Is open after a short remount. Use this to load draft data or prepare the object shown in Content.

property.caption: On close
property.description: Action executed when the modal is fully closed: header Close, dock tab Close, Escape key (topmost maximized modal only), overlay click if enabled, or Is open set to false. Use this to clear draft data, commit, or refresh lists. Not fired on minimize.

property.caption: On minimize
property.description: Action executed when the user clicks Minimize in the panel header. The modal becomes a dock tab; Content stays mounted so form values are not lost. Is open remains true (the modal is still logically open). Use this for analytics or soft-save if needed.

property.caption: On maximize
property.description: Action executed when the user restores the modal from a dock tab (Maximize on the tab). Not fired on the initial open from closed. Use this to refresh Content or refocus a field after restore.
